review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security features related to system monitoring and intrusion detection. However, some naming improvements were necessary for clarity and consistency with Kubernetes terminology. The use of audit logging and admission controllers is appropriate for detecting and preventing suspicious activities. Service mesh integration for security monitoring is also a valid approach.
naming_improvements: Use 'audit_logging' instead of 'audit' for clarity in k8s_audit_intrusion_detection_enabled
Use 'security_monitoring' instead of 'intrusion_detection' for service mesh integration to better reflect the function's purpose
title: System Monitoring - System monitoring | system - wide intrusion detection system connect and configu
step1_reasoning: The control SI-4(1) focuses on system-wide intrusion detection. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to enabling audit logs for detecting suspicious activities, using admission controllers to enforce security policies, and integrating with service mesh solutions that offer intrusion detection capabilities. While Kubernetes does not have a built-in intrusion detection system like cloud providers, these functions represent the closest equivalents.
